name: SymphonyAI
tagline: SAS wins on proven enterprise scale, regulatory depth, and integrated platform

overview.whatTheyDo: AI-native financial crime detection platform combining AML, fraud, and compliance capabilities. Acquired NICE Actimize's financial crime division (rebranded as Sensa). Focuses on AI-driven investigation automation and predictive analytics for financial crime.
overview.sweetSpot: Mid-to-large banks wanting AI-native AML/fraud with investigation automation. Active in APAC markets post-NICE acquisition. Positioned as next-gen alternative to legacy AML systems.
overview.typicalBuyer: AMLO, Head of Financial Crimes, Head of Compliance, CTO evaluating AI transformation
overview.phPresence: Growing APAC presence from Singapore hub. Acquired NICE Actimize customers inherit the relationship. Active marketing in ASEAN banking compliance conferences. Some inherited PH bank relationships from NICE portfolio.
overview.recentMomentum: Acquisition of NICE Actimize financial crime unit, Sensa AI platform launch, investigation automation AI (generative AI for compliance), aggressive APAC expansion, new leadership team focused on growth.
overview.pricingModel: Enterprise licensing based on entity volume and modules. Premium pricing for AI/automation features. Migration incentives for NICE Actimize customers to upgrade to Sensa platform.

whereWeWin.headline: Proven Enterprise Scale and Stability
whereWeWin.explanation: SAS has 50 years of enterprise deployment experience with proven scale at national levels. SymphonyAI is still integrating the NICE acquisition — platform migration uncertainty creates risk for customers. SAS is a known, stable platform.
whereWeWin.proofPoint: SAS processes fraud/AML for entire national banking systems. SymphonyAI is still consolidating acquired products into a unified platform — migration timelines uncertain.

whereWeWin.headline: Integrated Platform (Fraud + Risk + Analytics)
whereWeWin.explanation: SAS provides fraud, AML, credit risk, operational risk, AND enterprise analytics in one platform. SymphonyAI focuses on financial crime only — customers still need separate risk and analytics tools. SAS eliminates platform silos.
whereWeWin.proofPoint: SAS platform eliminates 3-5 point solutions, reducing integration costs and providing unified data model across fraud, risk, and compliance.

whereTheyWin.whyItMatters: SymphonyAI's Sensa platform uses generative AI to automate compliance investigation workflows — auto-generating SARs, summarizing cases, and recommending actions. This reduces analyst workload significantly.
whereTheyWin.howToNeutralize: SAS is investing in Gen AI for compliance workflows too. Ask about production readiness: how many banks are actually using their Gen AI in production vs. demos? Also, BSP/AMLC may have concerns about AI-generated SARs without human review.

whereTheyWin.whyItMatters: SymphonyAI inherited existing NICE Actimize customer relationships. Banks already on NICE may be encouraged to upgrade to Sensa rather than switch to a different vendor entirely.
whereTheyWin.howToNeutralize: Platform migration from NICE to Sensa is itself a major project — comparable effort to evaluating SAS. If they're going to migrate anyway, they should evaluate all options. Position SAS as lower-risk migration with proven platform.

features.notes: Both strong; SymphonyAI inherited NICE AML capabilities
Both capable; SAS broader algorithm library
SymphonyAI ahead on Gen AI for compliance; SAS building
Both ML-native with model training capabilities
SAS deeper; SymphonyAI building from NICE foundation
Not in SymphonyAI scope — financial crime only
SAS is full platform; SymphonyAI is point solution
SAS Model Manager; SymphonyAI has basic model tracking
SAS proven; SymphonyAI inheriting NICE templates
Both mature case management; SymphonyAI inherits NICE strength

landmines.question: What's the migration timeline and risk from NICE Actimize to the Sensa platform? How long until it's fully production-ready?
landmines.expectedAnswer: Uncertainty about migration timelines and feature parity between old NICE and new Sensa
landmines.whatToSayNext: If you're evaluating a platform migration anyway, why not evaluate all options? SAS offers a proven, integrated platform without the acquisition integration risk. Let us show you a lower-risk path.

landmines.question: Beyond AML and fraud, what are your requirements for credit risk, operational risk, and enterprise analytics?
landmines.expectedAnswer: They need broader capabilities than just financial crime
landmines.whatToSayNext: SAS is the only platform that covers fraud, AML, credit risk, operational risk, AND enterprise analytics. With SymphonyAI, you'd still need 2-3 additional platforms for those use cases.

objections.objection: SymphonyAI has more advanced AI for compliance automation
objections.whyTheySayIt: Their Gen AI marketing for investigation automation is compelling. Compliance teams want workload reduction.
objections.response: AI investigation assistance is valuable, and SAS is investing in this space. But ask: is their Gen AI production-proven at regulated Philippine banks, or is it still in pilot? BSP and AMLC have specific requirements about human review of SARs. The most impactful AI for compliance isn't generating reports — it's reducing false positives so your team reviews fewer cases. SAS ML reduces alerts by 50-70%.
proofPoints: SAS ML reduces false positives 50-70% — the most impactful compliance automation
BSP/AMLC require human review of suspicious activity — full automation has regulatory risk
SAS Gen AI capabilities in active development for compliance workflows
objections.whatNotToSay: Don't dismiss their AI capabilities — they're genuinely innovative. Focus on production readiness and regulatory acceptance.

objections.objection: We're already on NICE so upgrading to SymphonyAI Sensa is easier
objections.whyTheySayIt: Perceived lower migration risk staying with same vendor lineage.
objections.response: Migrating from NICE to Sensa is actually a significant platform change — new architecture, new UI, new integrations. The effort is comparable to evaluating SAS. Since you're investing in migration either way, shouldn't you evaluate the best platform for the next decade? SAS offers broader capabilities without acquisition integration uncertainty.
proofPoints: NICE to Sensa migration is a full re-platform, not a simple upgrade
Multiple banks have moved from NICE ecosystem to SAS successfully
SAS platform is stable — no acquisition integration risk

phContext.localPresence: Growing APAC presence from Singapore. Inherited NICE Actimize relationships in PH. Active in ASEAN compliance conferences. Some PH banks on legacy NICE may be approached for Sensa upgrade.
knownCustomers: Inherited NICE Actimize PH relationships (BPI, Security Bank)
Engaging PH banks on Sensa platform upgrade
Active marketing at PH compliance events
phContext.localPricing: Enterprise licensing through Singapore. Premium pricing similar to NICE Actimize levels. Migration incentives for existing NICE customers. USD-denominated contracts.
regulatoryGaps: Platform still integrating post-acquisition — stability concerns
Gen AI for compliance not yet proven with BSP/AMLC requirements
No credit risk or broader analytics capabilities
Migration from NICE to Sensa still in progress — feature gaps possible

partnerWhen: Never — direct competitor in AML/fraud space
competeWhen: Any AML or fraud detection opportunity
NICE Actimize customers evaluating upgrade to Sensa
Banks evaluating AI-native financial crime platforms

proofPoints.title: SAS recognized as Leader in AML and Fraud
proofPoints.detail: SAS consistently named Leader in Chartis RiskTech100 and Forrester Wave for fraud and AML — broader market recognition than SymphonyAI's newly integrated platform.

proofPoints.title: Bank migrated from NICE to SAS
proofPoints.detail: APAC bank moved from NICE Actimize to SAS for integrated fraud + AML + analytics — citing platform consolidation and better ML capabilities.

proofPoints.title: Integration cost reduction
proofPoints.detail: Banks on SAS unified platform report 40% lower integration costs vs. maintaining separate fraud, AML, and analytics vendors.
